articles.title: Airlines restore some Middle East flights as suspensions continue
articles.summary: Airlines restore some Middle East flights as suspensions continue Some airlines are restoring flights to parts of the Middle East as diplomatic efforts to end the war on Iran gather pace, though many carriers have kept suspensions in place. articles.title: Hormuz shipping may take months to return to normal, French shipping company says
articles.summary: Hormuz shipping may take months to return to normal, French shipping company says French shipping company CMA CGM hopes the shipping situation in the Strait of Hormuz will improve in the coming days, but expects a return to normal to take several months, its chairman and CEO Rodolphe Saade told French business daily Les Echos. Saade said the group has had several vessels stuck inside the Gulf since the US-Israeli war on Iran began at the end of February. In peacetime, about 20 percent of the world's oil passes through the strategic waterway.

articles.title: Oil prices fall as US leaders strike positive note on Iran talks
articles.summary: Oil prices fall as US leaders strike positive note on Iran talks Oil prices dropped by more than 1 percent as markets reacted to positive comments from US leaders on talks between Washington and Tehran. Brent futures fell $1.38, or 1.89 percent, to $71.57 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate crude lost 92 cents, or 1.32 percent, to $68.58 a barrel, according to Reuters. Both benchmarks closed at their lowest levels since March. As we have been reporting, Trump and Vance earlier said talks were going "very well" and "extremely well", respectively.

articles.title: Iranian parliament speaker says IAEA barred from bombed nuclear sites
articles.summary: Iranian parliament speaker says IAEA barred from bombed nuclear sites Iranian parliament speaker and negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf says inspections of nuclear sites that have been bombed and damaged are "not allowed under any circumstances". Speaking to Iran's Student News Network, Ghalibaf said the International Atomic Energy Agency has only been granted access to two sites in Iran, including the Bushehr power plant. "The access is only to this extent and we are committed to it," he said.

articles.title: Centcom leads regional security dialogue with Middle East military officials
articles.summary: Centcom leads regional security dialogue with Middle East military officials US Central Command has led a "security dialogue" alongside Bahrain's defence forces with military officials from across the Middle East. Representatives from Egypt, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, the United Arab Emirates and Yemen discussed "enhancing defence collaboration across the region", Centcom said in a post on X. Centcom Commander Admiral Brad Cooper said the discussions "underscored our shared commitment to regional security and stability".
